records.ety: [L. instillare, instillatum; pref. in- in + stillare to drop, fr. stilla a drop: cf. F. instiller. See Distill.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Instilled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Instilling.]

defs: To drop in; to pour in drop by drop.
quotes: That starlight dews All silently their tears of love instill.

defs: To infuse (knowledge or attitudes) into the mind of another, slowly or gradually; to impart gradually; to cause to be imbibed.
quotes: How hast thou instilled Thy malice into thousands.
